notes: Layer boundaries hold: routes delegate to service, service delegates to repo, repo is the only session-touching layer. count_unparseable_codes lives exclusively in app/repositories (T19 guards reimplementation). All five NFRs satisfied: NFR-F6-S2-1 probed by T15-T18 against seeded conforming/nonconforming rows; NFR-F6-S2-2 enforced by T20 (real PostgreSQL branch, no mocks); NFR-F6-S1-4/S1-5 carried through (real-branch DB, config-in-env, no hardcoded DSN); NFR-F6-S1-2 N/A (quantity untouched). The module-level function pattern for count_unparseable_codes (vs a class method on StockRepository) is a minor stylistic inconsistency but not a rubric violation -- the probe is intentionally DBA-facing with a bare Session parameter and the fitness test guards its placement. No duplicate class definitions, no inline HTML rendering, no import-time build coupling. No concrete rubric-cited improvement warranted.
